mnemonics.title: Quick Sort Steps
mnemonics.content: PICK a pivot, PARTITION around it, RECURSIVELY sort the parts. Remember: P-R-P - Pick, Recurse, Pivot placement!

mnemonics.title: Quick Sort vs Merge Sort
mnemonics.content: QUICK: QUick = Partition, In-place, Conquer, Handle; Stable: NO. MERGE: MErge = Middle, Extra space, Recurse, Guaranteed O(nlogn)

acronyms.expansion: Last Element Moves Under TO Underscore - the partition scheme where pivot is placed at end
acronyms.usage: Remember: Lomuto scheme uses last element as pivot and 'i' tracks smaller elements boundary

acronyms.expansion: HOARE uses two pointers that ARe Equal - two pointers approach
acronyms.usage: Hoare partition uses two pointers i and j moving towards each other

visualTips.visualization: Think of pivot as a 'divider' in a bowling alley. Balls (smaller elements) go left, bags (larger elements) go right. The pivot finds its lane (position).
Draw the recursion as a tree. Balanced partitions = balanced binary tree (O(log n) depth). Unbalanced = linked list (O(n) depth).

analogies.analogy: Choosing a pivot is like choosing a 'captain' to divide teams. Good captain = balanced teams (fast). Bad captain = one team empty (slow).
Partitioning is like organizing books on a shelf: pivot is like a 'reference book'. All smaller books go to its left, larger books to its right - the reference book finds its permanent spot.
